title: Polish Pork Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew made with tender pork and rich polish sauerkraut.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the pork on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the caraway seeds, salt, and pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sauerkraut, broth, and beer to the pot (if using).
Return the pork to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
